sections.paragraph:
- Original: Πούδης

- Transliteration: Poudes

- Phonetic: poo'-dace

- Definition: Pudens = "modest"

1. a Christian friend of Timothy at Rome, and may have been one of the seventy disciples

- Origin: of Latin origin

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Masculine

- Strong's: Of Latin origin; modest; Pudes (that is Pudens) a Christian: - Pudens.
